Card Title: M1 - Define Team Roles and Working Agreement
Description: Assign the Scrum Master and Product Owner, agree on team norms, and document branch, pull, and merge expectations.
Create two realistic personas, including goals, frustrations, and why they are the most important users to design for.
Map how users currently manage donations and resident care, and identify major pain points the product should solve.

Card Title: M4 - Write Problem Statement and Product Goal
Description: Define the specific business problem, user problem, and product goal the team will use to guide decisions.
Classify requirements into Must, Should, Could, and Won't items, add at least five nice-to-have ideas, and identify one feature not being built.

Card Title: M6 - Set Up Product Backlog and Sprint Boards
Description: Create the Trello product backlog, add estimates, and organize the Monday through Thursday sprint structure.

Card Title: M7 - Profile the Raw CSV Dataset
Description: Review the 17 CSV files, identify key tables and relationships, and note any missing data, inconsistencies, or fields the app will rely on most.
Create desktop wireframes for the three most important screens, such as landing page, dashboard, and caseload page.

Card Title: T1 - Explore and Choose UI Direction
Description: Generate AI UI concepts for the main screens, evaluate them, and document the final design choice with rationale.

Card Title: T2 - Build Shared Frontend Layout and Navigation
Description: Create the app shell, shared navigation, footer, and consistent styling structure for public and authenticated pages.
Implement the public homepage with organization overview, trust-building content, and clear calls to action.
Build the donor-facing impact page showing anonymized, aggregated outcomes and resource-use information.
Implement the login page with validation, error handling, and redirect behavior for authenticated users.

Card Title: T6 - Implement Authentication and Password Policy
Description: Configure ASP.NET Identity auth and enforce the class-required password policy of minimum 14 characters without extra composition requirements.
Restrict access by role so Admin can create, update, and delete, Employee has limited staff access, and Donor sees only donor-appropriate data.

Card Title: T8 - Build Privacy Policy and Cookie Consent
Description: Publish a tailored privacy policy and implement a functional cookie consent banner aligned with the grading rubric.
Implement the staff command-center dashboard with key metrics such as active residents, donation activity, and upcoming conferences.

Card Title: W2 - Build Donor and Supporter CRM
Description: Build the supporter management page with search, detail viewing, and create or update functionality for staff and admins.
Support recording and reviewing contributions across monetary and other contribution types tied to supporter records.
Add the ability to view or manage how donations are allocated across safehouses and program areas.

Card Title: W5 - Build Resident Intake and Profile Management
Description: Create or expand resident profiles with core demographic, case, and admission data needed for case management.
Implement filtering and search for residents by status, safehouse, case category, and assigned social worker.
API
Owner: Deploy one working end-to-end slice to the cloud and verify that submitted data persists in the live database.

Card Title: W8 - Gather User Feedback and Iterate
Description: Show the current product to a real user, capture five specific pieces of feedback, and document what should be changed next.
Implement chronological counseling note entry and review, including emotional state, interventions, narrative, and follow-up fields.
Build the workflow for recording home visit details, observations, family cooperation, safety concerns, and follow-up actions.
Add resident case conference history and upcoming conference tracking to support planning and follow-up.
Create reports for donations, resident outcomes, safehouse performance, and reintegration-related trends.

Card Title: TH5 - Add OKR Metric to the App
Description: Choose one meaningful product success metric, display it in the app, and document why it matters most.

Card Title: TH6 - Complete ML Pipeline and App Integration
Description: Finalize notebooks, export predictions, import them through the API, and display the results in the ML Insights or Action Center pages.
Accessibility
Owner: Fix accessibility issues, improve mobile layouts, and test the main user flows across public, admin, employee, and donor experiences.

Card Title: TH8 - Final Security Proof and Submission Prep
Description: Verify secrets handling, HTTPS and redirect behavior, collect screenshots and evidence for the video, and complete the retrospective and final submission checklist.
